a.tlua(16,7): error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{}'.
b.tlua(10,8): error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{ fixed: number; }'.
b.tlua(16,1): error TS7022: 'Paren' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
b.tlua(19,1): error TS7022: '1' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
b.tlua(19,14): error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type '"1"' can't be used to index type '{ 1: any; }'.
  Property '1' does not exist on type '{ 1: any; }'.
b.tlua(22,1): error TS7022: 'Chain' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
b.tlua(25,11): error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{}'.
b.tlua(30,1): error TS7022: 'ReadInside' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
b.tlua(32,19): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'LocalSelf'.
b.tlua(35,12): error TS2454: Variable 'Declared' is used before being assigned.
b.tlua(40,9): error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{ base: number; }'.


==== a.tlua (1 errors) ====
    G = G or {};
    G.fromA = 1;
    local fromB: string = G.fromB;
    
    local M = {};
    M.sub = M.sub or {};
    M.sub.value = true;
    local value: boolean = M.sub.value;
    
    local A = {};
    A.k = A["k"] or {};
    A.k.value = 1;
    local mixedKey: number = A["k"].value;
    
    Mixed = Mixed or {};
    Mixed.extra = 1;
          ~~~~~
!!! error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{}'.
    
    // An implicit global as the base of a nested member guard: the base read
    // `GNested` inside `GNested.sub` must resolve like any other global, not
    // trip used-before-assigned.
    GNested = {};
    GNested.sub = GNested.sub or {};
    GNested.sub.deep = 1;
    local deep: number = GNested.sub.deep;
    
==== b.tlua (10 errors) ====
    G = G ?? {};
    G.fromB = "b";
    local fromA: number = G.fromA;
    
    local function make(): { fixed: number }
      return { fixed = 1 };
    end
    
    Closed = Closed or make();
    Closed.extra = true;
           ~~~~~
!!! error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{ fixed: number; }'.
    
    Mixed = make();
    
    // Parentheses make this an ordinary self-referential assignment rather than
    // the exact defaulted-guard idiom.
    Paren = (Paren) or {};
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS7022: 'Paren' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
    
    local Numeric = {};
    Numeric[1] = Numeric["1"] or {};
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS7022: '1' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type '"1"' can't be used to index type '{ 1: any; }'.
!!! error TS7053:   Property '1' does not exist on type '{ 1: any; }'.
    
    local Other = {};
    Chain = Chain or Other or {};
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS7022: 'Chain' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
    
    Different = Other or {};
    Different.extra = true;
              ~~~~~
!!! error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{}'.
    
    local function keep(value: any): {}
      return {};
    end
    ReadInside = ReadInside or keep(ReadInside);
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS7022: 'ReadInside' implicitly has type 'any' because it does not have a type annotation and is referenced directly or indirectly in its own initializer.
    
    local LocalSelf = LocalSelf or {};
                      ~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS2304: Cannot find name 'LocalSelf'.
    
    local Declared: {};
    Declared = Declared or {};
               ~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS2454: Variable 'Declared' is used before being assigned.
    
    local Outer = { base = 1 };
    do
      local Outer = Outer or {};
      Outer.extra = true;
            ~~~~~
!!! error TS2339: Property 'extra' does not exist on type '{ base: number; }'.
